title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Two men, Anthony Shearrion and Arkie Swinson, were charged with assault after a parking incident in Dorchester, Boston.
The victim, who hit the suspects' car while parking, was reportedly beaten after the suspects followed him.
The suspects pleaded not guilty and were held at Suffolk County Jail, with a court date set for April 14.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox face challenges with their lineup, bullpen, and infield defense as the 2026 season begins.
Roman Anthony is expected to lead the offense while Boston addresses power and plate discipline issues.
The team navigates an outfield/DH surplus with Jarren Duran set as designated hitter for the opener.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Governor Maura Healey announced Massachusetts' public safety plan for the 2026 World Cup matches in Foxborough.
The plan, termed 'Match-Ready Massachusetts,' utilizes $76 million in federal funds for security and transportation improvements.
A Brazil vs. France friendly match will trial safety measures, with special trains and traffic adjustments planned.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The New England Revolution supports the White Stadium renovation, which faces a legal challenge due to its potential benefits to Massachusetts.
The renovation, backed by Boston Legacy FC, is a public-private partnership partially funded by Boston taxpayers at a cost of $135 million.
Opponents argue the development violates Article 97 of the Massachusetts Constitution, which protects public park and recreational land.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Medford, Maine man is in critical condition after being hit by a car on I-95 in Veazie.
He was assisting an 18-year-old Bangor woman who crashed due to snow when they were hit by another vehicle.
The 19-year-old driver from Old Town also lost control in the snow; all parties were taken to hospitals.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fire at 340 Main Road in Monterey, MA, is suspected to be intentionally set.
John Rohane, 60, was arrested after being located by State Police following the fire.
Rohane was hospitalized for reasons unrelated to the fire or his arrest; charges are pending.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: 11-year-old Labrador Tessa was rescued using pepperoni from a 12-foot sinkhole in Falmouth, Cape Cod.
The sinkhole formed due to an underground pipe leak that eroded the soil, firefighters reported.
Rescue crews used a pole leash to safely extract Tessa, who was found unharmed and enjoyed pepperoni afterward.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue, based in New England, is rehabilitating 16 neglected axolotls with missing limbs and bacterial infections.
The rescue, established in 2024, seeks donations to fund treatment for the axolotls, named after cheeses like Colby and Monterey.
Libertyland, the only U.S. nonprofit solely for axolotls, successfully rehomed over 100 axolotls in 2025, focusing on education and adoption.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters discusses how imperfections in relationships symbolize growth and personal connections.
A special screening of 'The Drama', starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, is happening in Somerville this Sunday.
Yowei Shaw's story on the Love Letters podcast explores lessons learned from hiding her husband, symbolized by a damaged table.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opens a 25-station salon providing cosmetology students hands-on training in hair, skin, and nail care.
The salon allows students to gain 1,000 hours of experience, preparing them for the state licensing exam for professional work.
Services performed by students are available to the public by appointment, under instructor supervision, enhancing real-world training.
